props.title: Open Source Lightweight React JSON Schema Form Library for Mantine
props.description: Build dynamic, reusable forms from JSON schema with Mantine components — optimized for bundle size, tree-shaking, and developer experience.

props.title: Why Choose WorkflowEngine Core with Mantine

features.title: Modern Design System
features.text: Mantine offers clean, modern components with excellent attention to detail. Your forms will look professional out of the box.

features.title: MIT Licensed
features.text: Both WorkflowEngine Core and the Mantine components package are MIT licensed. Use them freely in commercial and personal projects.

features.title: Rich Component Set
features.text: TextInput, NumberInput, Select, MultiSelect, Checkbox, Radio, Switch, Slider, DatePicker, ColorPicker, and more.

features.title: Excellent DX
features.text: Mantine is known for its developer experience. Great TypeScript support, intuitive APIs, and comprehensive documentation.

features.title: Accessible by Default
features.text: Mantine components are built with accessibility in mind. ARIA attributes, keyboard navigation, and screen reader support included.

features.title: TypeScript Support
features.text: Full TypeScript definitions for all components. Get autocomplete and type checking in your IDE.

features.title: Flexible Styling
features.text: Use Mantine's CSS-in-JS styling, CSS modules, or Tailwind CSS. Style your forms however you prefer.

features.title: Form Validation
features.text: Built-in validation with Mantine-styled error messages. Works with WorkflowEngine's validation system and third-party libraries.

features.title: RTL Support
features.text: Mantine supports RTL languages. Build forms for Arabic, Hebrew, and other RTL locales with ease.

props.title: How WorkflowEngine Core fits into your stack
props.description: WorkflowEngine Core maps a JSON form definition to a dynamic HTML form and lets you plug in your own UI components — or use the ready-made Mantine pack.

items.title: WorkflowEngine Core renders UI through Mantine view

props.title: Features built for real-world Mantine apps

features.title: UI-agnostic core
features.text: Keep the renderer independent from UI. Swap themes when needed.

features.title: Reusable JSON schemas
features.text: Copy, version, and reuse forms across applications and teams.

features.title: Modern stack friendly
features.text: Works smoothly with Next.js, Remix, and other React setups.

features.title: Multiple themes supported
features.text: MUI, shadcn/ui, React Suite, Mantine — with more on the way.

features.title: Open-source core + optional drag and drop form builder
features.text: Start with the free MIT core and scale to a drag-and-drop workflow when needed.

props.title: Built for API-driven products and internal tools

faqItems.question: Is WorkflowEngine Core open source?
faqItems.answer: Yes. The core library is MIT-licensed, which is suitable for commercial use.

faqItems.question: How do I use it with Mantine?
faqItems.answer: Install @react-form-builder/components-mantine and render forms with FormViewer using the provided Mantine view.

faqItems.question: Do I need a visual drag-and-drop builder?
faqItems.answer: No. You can render and manage forms purely from JSON. A visual builder can be added later if your workflow needs it.

faqItems.question: Can I store form schemas in a database and deliver them via API?
faqItems.answer: Yes — the JSON schema can be loaded from any source (file, DB, CMS, API) and rendered in the client.

faqItems.question: Does it work with Next.js / Remix?
faqItems.answer: Yes — it's designed to work smoothly with modern React stacks, including Next.js and Remix.

faqItems.question: What UI themes are supported?
faqItems.answer: MUI, shadcn/ui, React Suite, Mantine are listed as supported themes, with more coming soon.

faqItems.question: How small is the bundle?
faqItems.answer: The repository provides a benchmark example: ~188.54 KB gzip for WorkflowEngine Core (JS + CSS).

faqItems.question: Can I use my own Mantine-based components?
faqItems.answer: Yes — the core is designed to render using your components; the Mantine pack is a ready-made option.

props.title: Ready to build schema-driven Mantine forms?
props.description: Start with the MIT-licensed core, render your first form, and keep your UI consistent with Mantine.
